_info: PenToolkit — Web Application Test Payload Database v1.0
_description: Detection payloads for OWASP Top 10 testing. These are DETECTION probes only — they look for vulnerability indicators without exploiting them.
_important: These payloads are designed to cause observable differences in responses (timing, error messages, redirects) that INDICATE a vulnerability exists. They do not extract data or cause harm.

sql_injection.description: SQL Injection — attacker tricks database into running unintended queries

detection_payloads.desc: Single quote — causes SQL syntax error if not sanitised
Double quote — alternative syntax error trigger

error_signatures: you have an error in your sql syntax
microsoft ole db provider for sql server
supplied argument is not a valid mysql
sql_injection.recommendation: Use parameterised queries (prepared statements) for ALL database operations. NEVER concatenate user input into SQL strings. Use an ORM. Apply input validation and output encoding.

xss.description: Cross-Site Scripting — attacker injects malicious scripts that run in victims' browsers
xss.detection_method: Check if payload appears unencoded in response body
xss.recommendation: Encode ALL output before rendering in HTML. Use Content-Security-Policy headers. Never trust user-supplied data. Use a template engine that auto-escapes output.

path_traversal.description: Path Traversal — attacker accesses files outside the intended web directory
path_traversal.recommendation: Validate and sanitise all file path inputs. Use a whitelist of allowed paths. Never concatenate user input with file system paths. Chroot web processes where possible.

open_redirect.description: Open Redirect — attacker uses the site to redirect victims to malicious URLs
open_redirect.recommendation: Avoid redirect parameters entirely if possible. If required, use a whitelist of allowed redirect destinations. Validate that redirect URLs belong to your own domain.

sensitive_files.description: Sensitive File Exposure — configuration and backup files left accessible

paths.desc: Environment config — often contains database passwords and API keys
Git repository config — may expose source code
Git HEAD file — confirms git repo exposure
PHP info page — reveals server configuration
Apache server status — reveals requests and IPs
Robots file — may reveal hidden paths
Apache config — may reveal security rules
macOS folder metadata — reveals directory structure
Swagger API docs — reveals all endpoints
Spring Boot actuator — exposes app internals
Spring Boot env endpoint — exposes config

security_headers.description: HTTP Security Headers — browser instructions for safe behaviour

required_headers.description: Prevents Cross-Site Scripting by defining trusted content sources
required_headers.recommendation: Add a Content-Security-Policy header. Start with: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'

required_headers.description: Prevents your site from being embedded in iframes (clickjacking protection)
required_headers.recommendation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY (or SAMEORIGIN if you embed your own site)

required_headers.description: Prevents browsers from guessing content types (MIME sniffing attacks)
Forces browsers to always use HTTPS for this domain
Controls what referrer information is sent with requests
Controls which browser features the page can use (camera, microphone, etc.)

cors.description: CORS Misconfiguration — overly permissive cross-origin resource sharing

dangerous_responses.desc: Wildcard CORS — any origin can read responses
Null origin allowed — exploitable via sandboxed iframes

cors.recommendation: Never use Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * for authenticated endpoints. Whitelist specific trusted origins only.

info_disclosure.description: Information disclosure through error messages and headers
info_disclosure.recommendation: Disable detailed error messages in production. Use custom error pages. Log errors server-side only.
